preamble: The shift from a voiceless lateral fricative [ɬ] to a voiceless alveolar sibilant [s] is a common form of sibilantization, where the lateral airflow is lost in favor of a central grooved articulation.

examples.note: Historical merger of the lateral fricative into the sibilant series.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
